tasks.title: Task #2d: Taxonomy Mapper (Phase 4)
tasks.description:
Build the course recommendation engine that maps student aptitude/interest signals to personalized learning pathways. Converts AI signals into a curated list of courses.

## What's Included:
- Load skill taxonomy from courses-v2.html (taxonomy-parser.js): parse courses-v2.html, extract skill taxonomy (COVERAGE/EVIDENCE matrices), build in-memory map of skills → courses
- Taxonomy mapper function (taxonomy-mapper.js): takes aptitude score + interest tags, queries taxonomy, recommends primary course + 2-3 follow-up courses based on skill overlap, returns structured pathway object
- Integrate mapper into assessment completion (assessment-chat.js): after AI completes and signals extracted (Phase 3), call mapper, store pathway for Phase 2.3 (QR display) and Phase 6 (persistence)

## Files Modified:
- ai-academy/js/taxonomy-parser.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/taxonomy-mapper.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/assessment-chat.js (extend)

## Effort: ~2 days
## Blocker: Task #8 (AI Engine output required)
## Dependencies: Task #8 (needs aptitude/interest signals)

## Success Criteria:
✅ Taxonomy parses from courses-v2.html correctly
✅ Mapper generates relevant course pathways
✅ Aptitude/interest signals align with recommended courses
✅ Pathway includes primary + follow-up courses with skill progression
✅ Pathway is available to Phase 6 and stored in learner record

tasks.title: Task #2e: Integration & UX (Phases 5-6)
tasks.description:
Wire assessment into the homepage and student dashboard. Makes assessment discoverable and displays personalized pathways to students after completion.

## What's Included:
- Homepage integration (Phase 5): add "Take Assessment" button/link to homepage (visible before login, alongside "Explore Courses"); route to assessment.html
- Conditional logic on homepage: check if learner has taken assessment (Firestore assessment_results exists); show "View Pathway" instead of "Take Assessment" if true; offer "Re-take Assessment" option
- Student page pathway section (Phase 6): add new "Recommended Pathway" section on students.html displaying course list from learner record
- Pathway display & navigation: render pathway courses as clickable cards (title, description, difficulty); clicking routes to course page; show "Start Next Course" CTA
- QR recovery UI on student page: add "Lost your learner ID?" section with link to QR recovery flow; warn that recovery requires saved QR code or recovery token

## Files Modified:
- ai-academy/index.html (add assessment button + conditional logic)
- ai-academy/js/homepage.js (new or extend)
- ai-academy/students.html (add pathway section + QR recovery UI)
- ai-academy/css/homepage.css (extend or new)
- ai-academy/css/students.css (extend or new)
- ai-academy/js/pathway-display.js (new)
- ai-academy/js/qr-recovery.js (extend from #7)

## Effort: ~1.5 days
## Blocker: Tasks #6, #7, #9
## Dependencies: #6 (Foundation), #7 (Chat UI), #9 (Mapper with pathway data)

## Success Criteria:
✅ "Take Assessment" button visible on homepage
✅ Homepage shows "View Pathway" if assessment already taken
✅ Student page displays recommended pathway with courses
✅ Courses are clickable and route to course pages
✅ QR recovery option is visible and functional

tasks.title: Consolidate Firebase client config into shared module
tasks.description:
firebase-helpers.js (introduced in Task #6) hardcodes the Firebase client config block (apiKey, projectId, appId, etc.), duplicating what is already present in students.html, transcript.html, and dashboard.html. Firebase client config is not a secret, but duplication creates a maintenance sync burden — a project change requires updating all copies.

Extract the config into a shared module (e.g. ai-academy/js/firebase-config.js) and import it from all consumers. Verify all pages still initialize Firebase correctly after the change.
